items.title: Roads set to reopen as clean-up continues in Exmouth

children.content: Visitors in Exmouth who got stuck in the town when Cyclone Narelle passed are now trying to find ways out of the town.

items.title: Apocalyptic scenes from Australia ahead of cyclone make worldwide news

children.content: Cyclone Narelle turned the sky over parts of Western Australia's north into an ominous red, sparking headlines across the globe.

image.altText: The streets of shark bay are cast in an eerie cloud of red due to TC Narelle

items.title: Exmouth beaches a 'graveyard' after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: In the popular tourist destination, it appears the category four cyclone has chewed up and spat out whole sections of the Ningaloo Reef onto dry land.

image.altText: A baby turtle lying dead on the sand surrounded by seaweed

items.title: Photos explore psychological drama of Australian rural life

children.content: Western Australian documentary photographer Brad Rimmer's beautiful, melancholy images of Wheatbelt life sprawl through Fremantle gallery.

gallery.altText: Darkened corridor with series of black and white images overlaid with deep colour
Black and white image of bare trees, overlaid with deep teal colour.
Young woman in hoodie stands outside in a scrubby landscape, with dark clouds behind her

items.title: Man charged after baby killed, woman and child injured as rideshare car hits tree

children.content: A seven-month-old child has died after a rideshare vehicle crashed into a tree in Perth's south-east, leaving two other passengers seriously injured.

image.altText: Lynwood crash

items.title: Green's IPL skipper questions Cricket Australia for not letting $3.8m man bowl

children.content: After becoming the most expensive overseas purchase in IPL history, Australian Cameron Green is apparently not allowed to bowl in Kolkata's opening IPL match.

image.altText: Cameron Green throws a cricket ball to himself at training.

items.title: Antarctic penguin species appears on WA beach in rare sighting

children.content: The king penguin, known for being the second-largest penguin species and living on ice-free parts of the sub-Antarctic, was rescued near Esperance on WA's south coast.

image.altText: Sub-Antarctic king penguin found on WA's south coast in rare sighting

gallery.altText: woman in blue polo and hat leaning by a penguin, she is reflected in a mirror behind her.
Penguin with its beak tucked in
Woman in blue polo holding a penguin as someone else tends to its foot.

thumbnail.altText: woman in blue polo and hat leaning by a penguin, she is reflected in a mirror behind her.
Penguin with its beak tucked in
Woman in blue polo holding a penguin as someone else tends to its foot.

items.title: Confronting images of 'unprecedented' outback flood after cyclone

children.content: An outback cattle station and tourist ranch in WA's north has felt the full force of Cyclone Narelle, with vision emerging of the trail of destruction left by the powerful wind and heavy rain.

image.altText: What appears to be a goat seeks shelter in a car while being pummelled with fast moving flood water.

articles.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys banana crops, fresh food growers hit hard

children.content: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

image.altText: A wide shot of a man wearing work uniform and walking through his banana plantation.

articles.title: Cyclone Narelle devastates WA tourist town, airport 'obliterated'

children.content: The northern WA tourist town of Exmouth has been left isolated and heavily damaged after Cyclone Narelle tore through the town.

articles.title: WA gas facilities, ports suffer major disruptions after cyclone

children.content: Gas giants Woodside, Santos and Chevron confirm they have suffered production outages at its gas as a result of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

image.altText: An aerial shot of the Gorgon gas project on the coast of Western Australia.

gallery.altText: A man paddleboards through a rural property

thumbnail.altText: A man paddleboards through a rural property

items.title: Emergency relief announced as cleanup begins for ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Structures have been destroyed and more than 1000 people remain without power as the cleanup begins in Western Australia following ex-tropical cyclone Narelle.

image.altText: A worker is repairing electricity transmission lines.

items.title: ASX down 0.8pc to follow Wall Street, oil up sharply as Houthis enter war

children.content: The ASX 200 has recovered some of its earlier losses to be down by just 0.8 per cent in Monday trade after investors dumped banks and tech stocks in the wake of Wall Street losses on Friday night. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Man points to ASX board, in red and black colours, in Sydney.

items.title: Vital Aboriginal records saved in the 70s by an astute WA archivist

children.content: Margaret Medcalf, who turns 100 this week, insisted that decades of Aboriginal welfare records be preserved in the state archives and not destroyed. Those documents have proved essential to connecting Aboriginal people with their family history.

image.altText: black and white photo of Margaret Medcalf in the archives in 1969

items.title: How to discuss additional work-from-home days with your employer

children.content: With rising fuel prices adding additional financial stress to household living costs, what are your rights when it comes to working from home — and what are the best ways to approach your employer about flexible work arrangements?

image.altText: A woman sits in front of a computer monitor at home, with her cat sitting on a windowsill

items.title: Consumer pain within 'weeks' if WA diesel supplies aren't resolved

children.content: Country business owners say they have worked to absorb price increases, but will be forced to pass on costs to consumers if the supply of fuel to regional WA isn't stabilised.

articles.title: Rising fuel costs force Australians to rethink Easter holidays

children.content: Rising fuel prices driven by the Middle East conflict are hitting the domestic tourism sector, with cancellations surging ahead of the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: A row of caravans.

articles.title: Rural families consider giving up sport and school runs as fuel prices soar

children.content: Rural and remote families who are spending as much as $500 a week on fuel amid the current oil crisis say they are preparing to make tough decisions about whether to make school runs and to go without sport and even health care.

image.altText: Two primary school children outside their rural school.

items.title: The best and worst things about being on reality TV, according to Guy Sebastian

children.content: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

image.altText: Guy Sebastian sits behind a table smiling. Behind him are shop mannequins and sale signs

items.title: Former AFL player Nick Stevens found guilty of dishonesty charges

children.content: A jury has found AFL player Nick Stevens guilty of deceptively charging families for swimming pools they never received.

image.altText: man walking outside with phone in hand

items.title: 36ers lodge complaint to NBL over Bogut's 'personal attacks' on owner

children.content: Drama from the spiteful championship series between the Adelaide 36ers and Sydney Kings goes off the court as the 36ers complain to the NBL about the conduct of Kings co-owner and assistant Andrew Bogut.

image.altText: Andrew Bogut points with his left hand

items.title: As universities cut courses, spending on consultants and contractors nears $2b

children.content: Consultancies have been accused of infiltrating universities and wasting scarce public funds on questionable advice about cutting courses and jobs.

image.altText: A student walks by several others sitting in a grassy area. Behind them is a modern glass building.

items.title: After 80 years, sleuths have unearthed a secret stash of early art and reunited them with family

children.content: A stash of more than 800 mysterious drawings has been revealed for the first time, rewriting the timeline of Aboriginal art and reconnecting families with long-lost relatives.

items.title: Decisions made more than a decade ago are coming home to roost

children.content: The early 2010s were the years of peak globalisation, when it seemed all trade barriers were on a one-way trip to elimination. That was before Donald Trump arrived and ruptured the global order.

items.title: Kerr and Carpenter hit the ground running for Chelsea in Asian Cup return

image.altText: Sam Kerr jumps on Sjoeke Nusken's back. Ellie Carpenter celebrates behind them.

items.title: Aussies cruise to ODI series win over West Indies with dominant second game

children.content: Australia takes an unassailable 2-0 lead over the West Indies with a cruisy 91-run victory in the second ODI at St Kitts' Warner Park.

items.title: Teen says single parent bias is leaving Australian families homeless

children.content: Hunter was plunged into homelessness when his family lost their rental. It is an increasingly similar story to other single parents finding themselves "lifelong renters".

items.title: 'I feel betrayed': New scam figures show Australians lost more than $2 billion

children.content: Australians lost more than $2 billion to scammers in 2025, the latest data from the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has revealed.

items.title: South Australia wins back-to-back Shield titles with stunning win over Victoria

children.content: South Australia has won back-to-back Sheffield Shield titles with a brilliant comeback victory over Victoria in the final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: South Australia bowler Henry Thornton jumps, shouts and punches the air to celebrate a Sheffield Shield wicket.

items.title: Haggis and traditional dancing in WA's outback

children.content: Scottish expats in WA's outback host traditional Ceilidh in a belated honouring of Burns Night.
